title: Generative Language Modeling for Automated Theorem Proving (GPT-f)
claim: Transformer LM + best-first search over Metamath; models 160M-1.5B trained, best held-out test pass 56.22% at 700M (policy+value, iteration 2, a=32/e=32/d=256); expert iteration = train value fn on positively/negatively annotated goals from its OWN verified proof searches; ~20k V100 GPU-hours PER sampling iteration, ~1k GPU-h per benchmark eval; first deep-learning proofs adopted into the Metamath library. Model-size/compute figures from ar5iv full text fetched 2026-07-11.

title: Proof Artifact Co-Training for Theorem Proving with Language Models
venue: ICLR 2022 (arXiv page shows no venue; venue [memory])
claim: Self-supervised co-training data extracted from Lean kernel-level proof terms raises held-out theorem success 32%->48% - proof ARTIFACTS (not just tactic steps) are cheap high-value training signal.

title: Formal Mathematics Statement Curriculum Learning
claim: Expert iteration (proof search interleaved with learning) dramatically outperforms proof-search-only AT EQUIVALENT COMPUTE BUDGET - the compute-matched training-time win for sound-verifier-filtered self-training; expert iteration finds an implicit difficulty curriculum from unordered statement sets without ground-truth proofs; then-SOTA miniF2F.

title: HyperTree Proof Search for Neural Theorem Proving (Evariste)
venue: NeurIPS 2022 [memory]; arXiv abs page shows no venue
claim: HTPS + online training from its own proof searches: 82.6% held-out Metamath (65.4% search-only) vs GPT-f 56.5% prior SOTA; Lean miniF2F-curriculum 31%->42%. Comparison NOT stated compute-matched; search budgets large.

title: Thor: Wielding Hammers to Integrate Language Models and Automated Theorem Provers
venue: NeurIPS 2022 [memory]; arXiv abs page shows no venue
claim: Division of labour: hammers (classical ATPs) do premise selection, LM does the rest - PISA success 39%->57%, +8.2% problems neither component solves alone; competitive miniF2F at significantly smaller compute budget. Precedent for splitting labour between neural proposer and deterministic machinery along capability lines.

title: miniF2F: a cross-system benchmark for formal Olympiad-level mathematics
claim: 488 formal statements (AIME/AMC/IMO/HS-UG coursework) targeting Metamath, Lean, Isabelle (partial), HOL Light (partial); the field's standard NTP yardstick.

title: LeanDojo: Theorem Proving with Retrieval-Augmented Language Models (ReProver)
claim: ReProver = google/byt5-small 299M + retrieval over premises; LeanDojo Benchmark Pass@1: 51.2% random / 26.3% novel-premises vs no-retrieval SAME-MODEL ablation 47.6%/23.2% (a genuinely matched win) vs GPT-4 29.0%/7.4% (unmatched accounting: zero-shot vs finetuned); miniF2F 26.5% Pass@1, ProofNet 13.8%; trains in 5 days on one A100 (~1 GPU-week) vs prior work's thousands of GPU-days. THE <=2B-scale NTP anchor. Full-text figures via ar5iv fetch 2026-07-11.

title: Graph2Tac: Online Representation Learning of Formal Math Concepts
venue: ICML 2024 [memory]; arXiv abs page shows cs.LG only
claim: GNN (~10M-scale [memory: parameter count not on abs page]) with hierarchical representations of NEW definitions, learned online in Coq: 1.5x theorems solved over offline baseline; with k-NN co-solver outperforms CoqHammer, Proverbot9001, and a transformer baseline by >=1.48x. Compute/wallclock-matching of comparisons not stated. Evidence that tiny non-transformer provers with definition-online-learning beat bigger offline ones in ITP settings.

title: DeepSeek-Prover-V1.5: Harnessing Proof Assistant Feedback for Reinforcement Learning and Monte-Carlo Tree Search
venue: ICLR 2025 [memory]; arXiv abs page shows no venue
claim: RLPAF (RL from proof-assistant feedback: Lean verification as reward) after SFT + RMaxTS intrinsic-reward MCTS: miniF2F-test 63.5%, ProofNet 25.3% (7B [memory: size not on abs page]). The executor-as-RL-reward precedent at prover scale.

title: Goedel-Prover-V2: Scaling Formal Theorem Proving with Scaffolded Data Synthesis and Self-Correction
claim: 8B model 84.6% pass@32 miniF2F, beating DeepSeek-Prover-V2-671B under the SAME pass@32 metric at 80x fewer params (identical total budget not explicitly stated); 32B 88.1% pass@32 / 90.4% with self-correction; methods: scaffolded (difficulty-laddered) synthetic task generation, verifier-guided self-correction on Lean compiler feedback, checkpoint averaging. Small-beats-huge via recipe+verifier, not parameters.

title: Pythagoras-Prover: Advancing Efficient Formal Proving via Augmented Lean Formalisation
claim: 4B autoregressive prover 86.1% pass@32 miniF2F-test, surpassing DeepSeek-Prover-V2-671B at pass@32 with ~167x fewer parameters; 32B 93.0% (open SOTA); stratified easy/medium/hard curriculum + self-distilled augmented formalisation corpus; built for practical compute budgets.

title: STP: Self-play LLM Theorem Provers with Iterative Conjecturing and Proving
claim: Names the expert-iteration PLATEAU: correct proofs are scarce so rewards sparsify on a fixed statement set; self-play conjecturer generates statements barely provable by the current prover; 51.3B Lean training tokens -> LeanWorkbook 28.5% (prior best 13.2%), miniF2F-test 65.0% pass@3200. The binding constraint in sound-verifier training loops is STATEMENT SUPPLY, not checking.

title: miniF2F-Lean Revisited: Reviewing Limitations and Charting a Path Forward
claim: More than half of original miniF2F problems have formal/informal misalignments (errors, simplifications, inconsistencies); corrected miniF2F-v2 released; end-to-end (understand->formalize->prove) accuracy 36% on v1 vs component-wise 97% autoformalization / 69% proving; best on v2 = 70%. All headline miniF2F SOTA numbers carry benchmark-quality risk.

title: Olympiad-level formal mathematical reasoning with reinforcement learning (AlphaProof)
claim: 3B encoder-decoder + AlphaZero-style RL against the Lean environment on ~80M auto-formalized problems (from ~1M NL problems); IMO 2024: 28/42 points (silver range, with AlphaGeometry 2), solved P1/P2/P6; Test-Time RL generates and learns from formal variants of a single hard problem. Compute: ~80,000 TPU-days main RL + ~100,000 TPU-days autoformalization - the maximally UNMATCHED-compute end of the verifier-loop family.

title: Solving olympiad geometry without human demonstrations (AlphaGeometry)
claim: 151M-param LM (excl. embeddings; 12 layers) trained from scratch on 100M engine-generated synthetic theorems+proofs, proposing auxiliary constructions to a deterministic symbolic engine (deductive database DD + algebraic reasoning AR); IMO-AG-30: 25/30 vs prior best 10 (18 for DD+AR+hand-heuristics); inference beam k=512, <=16 iterations, 4 GPU + 10,000 CPU workers. The flagship small-LM + deterministic-executor existence proof; the LM is small, the search is not.

title: Gold-medalist Performance in Solving Olympiad Geometry with AlphaGeometry2
claim: Gemini-based LM + faster symbolic engine + search-tree knowledge sharing: 84% on all IMO geometry 2000-2024 (was 54%); language coverage 66%->88%; part of the IMO 2024 silver system. Scaling the NEURAL side of a neurosymbolic pair kept paying off - the symbolic engine did not cap the system.

title: End-to-End Differentiable Proving
claim: Differentiable backward chaining with RBF-kernel soft unification over embeddings; beats ComplEx on 3/4 SMALL KBs (Countries, Nations, Kinship, UMLS) while inducing interpretable rules. Computation graph enumerates proof paths - the original does not scale beyond toy KBs (scalability limit acknowledged by its own successors, see gntp-2020).

title: Differentiable Reasoning on Large Knowledge Bases and Natural Language (Greedy NTPs)
claim: Makes NTP tractable by expanding only most-promising proof paths (orders-of-magnitude cheaper); performs ON PAR WITH NTPs at a fraction of cost and 'competitive' (not superior) link prediction on large KBs - i.e., scaling recovered tractability, not a matched-baseline WIN over dedicated link predictors.

title: Learning Reasoning Strategies in End-to-End Differentiable Proving (Conditional Theorem Provers)
claim: Learned rule-selection via gradient descent; SOTA on CLUTRR systematic generalization (train on small kinship graphs, test on larger) vs neural baselines - the one clean BENCHMARK win for differentiable proving (resource-matching of the comparison not established), achieved exactly where the task IS closed-world multi-hop rule application at tiny scale.

title: Learning Explanatory Rules from Noisy Data (dILP)
claim: Differentiable ILP robust to noise/ambiguity unlike classical ILP; explicitly memory-bound - bounded predicate counts and rule templates limit it to small domains. Rule LEARNING at kernel-rule scale is feasible; rule REASONING at KB scale is not, in this family.

title: DeepProbLog: Neural Probabilistic Logic Programming
claim: Neural predicates inside ProbLog with exact probabilistic inference in the training objective (executor-in-the-loss, module scale: MNIST-addition-class tasks); the canonical probabilistic-logic-in-the-loss system; exact WMC inference cost is the known scaling ceiling (successors Scallop/A-NESI exist to approximate it).

title: Scallop: A Language for Neurosymbolic Programming
venue: PLDI 2023 [memory]; arXiv abs page shows cs.PL only
claim: Datalog + provenance semirings for tunable approximate differentiable reasoning (top-k proofs); across 8 neurosymbolic apps: comparable-or-better accuracy than SOTA neural models with better runtime/data-efficiency/interpretability - module/app scale, not LM-scale, and 'comparable-or-superior' is the honest summary, not dominance.

title: A Semantic Loss Function for Deep Learning with Symbolic Knowledge
claim: Compile logical constraints (via circuits) into a differentiable loss bridging network outputs and symbolic specs; wins in semi-supervised classification + structured outputs (rankings, paths). The clean 'constraint-executor in the LOSS' primitive - proven at output-layer/module scale only.

title: Differentiation of Blackbox Combinatorial Solvers
claim: Exact solvers (Gurobi MIP, Blossom V, Dijkstra) embedded as layers with informative gradients via perturbed linearization - end-to-end training THROUGH an exact deterministic executor without softening it. Direct precedent that a discrete exact engine can sit inside a training loop; demonstrated on TSP/matching/shortest-path scale, never at LM scale with a logic engine.

title: The CLRS Algorithmic Reasoning Benchmark
claim: 30 textbook algorithms as graph tasks with intermediate-trajectory hints and OOD (larger-input) evaluation; unified the NAR field's evaluation. Baseline comparisons are WITHIN-FRAMEWORK (GNN processor variants), not vs matched general-purpose models.

title: A Generalist Neural Algorithmic Learner
claim: Single multi-task GNN processor (Triplet-GMPNN) over CLRS-30; improved average single-task performance by >20% over prior art via input-representation/training/processor fixes. The win is vs prior NAR processors on synthetic tasks - no external matched baseline.

title: Transformers meet Neural Algorithmic Reasoners (TransNAR)
claim: LM tokens cross-attend to a pretrained NAR's node embeddings; significant in- and out-of-distribution gains over Transformer-only on CLRS-Text. Abstract does not state parameter/compute-matching of the baseline nor the '20% absolute' magnitude (UNVERIFIED at that precision); requires the graph input at inference - a fusion prior-art datapoint for P3-LR-FUSE more than a deployable recipe.

title: The CLRS-Text Algorithmic Reasoning Language Benchmark
claim: Textual traces of CLRS-30 for LMs as generalist executors; confirms LMs' known weakness generalizing to inputs longer than training - the text-side NAR yardstick for any H-GU trace-trained variant.

title: Discrete Neural Algorithmic Reasoning
claim: Forcing execution trajectories into finite predefined discrete states (separating discrete/continuous flows) yields PERFECT test scores incl. OOD in single- and multi-task setups, with correctness provable on any test data - the strongest published support for 'hard symbolic interface at step 0 buys OOD transfer', on covered algorithmic tasks with state supervision.

title: Neural Algorithmic Reasoning
claim: The NAR blueprint: train processors to execute algorithms in latent space, then deploy on rich real inputs classical algorithms cannot touch. The real-input transfer leg of this blueprint remains thin in practice (see review body).

title: Exploring Length Generalization in Large Language Models
venue: NeurIPS 2022 [memory]; arXiv abs page shows no venue
claim: Naive finetuning shows substantial length-generalization deficiency REGARDLESS OF SCALE (parity/LEGO-class tasks); in-context + scratchpad composition helps. Trace-format supervision alone does not buy algorithmic extrapolation.

title: Transformers Can Achieve Length Generalization But Not Robustly
claim: With the right position encoding + data format, 2.5x length extrapolation on integer addition IS achievable - but fragile: heavily dependent on weight init and training-data order, high variance across seeds. Any H-GU sweep read must be powered for seed variance; single-seed direction reads are uninterpretable.

title: Transformers as Soft Reasoners over Language (RuleTaker)
claim: Transformers trained on NL rule theories reach 99% in-distribution on deduction, 95%+ on depths beyond training, and transfer zero-shot to hand-authored and paraphrased rulebases - bounded-depth closed-world deduction over stated rules is learnable at ~350M scale.

title: ProofWriter: Generating Implications, Proofs, and Abductive Statements over Natural Language
claim: Generates proofs (not just labels); ITERATIVE single-step implication generation generalizes to unseen proof depths and OOD theories (+9pt over prior on RuleTaker data); proofs reflect actual inference chain. Method template: one verified step at a time beats whole-proof generation for depth transfer.

title: On the Paradox of Learning to Reason from Data
venue: IJCAI 2023 [memory]; arXiv abs page shows no venue
claim: BERT trained on SimpleLogic reaches near-perfect in-distribution accuracy yet FAILS on other distributions of the SAME problem space - it learned statistical features inherent to logical reasoning data, not the reasoning function. The canonical shortcut-learning kill for naive trace/label distillation; OOD-within-problem-space splits are mandatory controls.

title: Language Models Are Greedy Reasoners: A Systematic Formal Analysis of Chain-of-Thought (PrOntoQA)
claim: Parsing CoT into formal proofs shows InstructGPT/GPT-3 make correct INDIVIDUAL deduction steps but fail at PROOF PLANNING - cannot systematically explore when multiple valid steps exist. Localizes the neural deficit to search/planning, exactly the part a deterministic engine or search harness can own.

title: STaR: Bootstrapping Reasoning With Reasoning
venue: NeurIPS 2022 [memory]; arXiv abs page shows no venue
claim: Generate rationales, keep only those yielding the CORRECT answer (+ rationalization from the answer for failures), finetune, iterate - GPT-J 6B matches a 30x larger model finetuned on CommonsenseQA. Correctness-filtered self-training with a WEAK verifier (answer match); a sound engine improves filter PRECISION on covered statements only - not strict dominance (coverage cost + formalization error remain).

title: Distilling Step-by-Step! Outperforming Larger Language Models with Less Training Data and Smaller Model Sizes
claim: Finetuned 770M T5 beats FEW-SHOT-PROMPTED 540B PaLM on benchmark tasks using rationale-augmented multi-task finetuning with 80% of data. ACCOUNTING FLAG: task-specific finetune vs few-shot generalist is NOT a matched comparison - it licenses 'small specialized can beat large generalist per task', nothing stronger.

title: Symbolic Chain-of-Thought Distillation: Small Models Can Also 'Think' Step-by-Step
claim: CoT distillation from a large teacher into 125M-1.3B students improves commonsense reasoning (supervised + few-shot, esp. challenge sets); MULTIPLE sampled chains per instance from the teacher is essential. CoT benefit is transferable INTO the 100M-2B band with dense sampling.

title: Language Models can be Logical Solvers (LoGiPT)
venue: NAACL Findings 2024 [memory]; arXiv abs page shows no venue
claim: Finetunes LMs directly on the REVEALED internal step traces of a deductive solver so the LM emulates solver reasoning without an NL->symbolic parsing step; outperforms solver-augmented LM pipelines (whose parse errors are fatal) and few-shot GPT-4/ChatGPT on ProofWriter/PrOntoQA-class benchmarks. The closest published shape to 'distill kernel proof traces into a small LM'.

title: Textbooks Are All You Need (phi-1)
claim: 1.3B model, 4 days x 8 A100s, 6B filtered web + 1B GPT-3.5-synthetic 'textbook' tokens -> HumanEval 50.6% / MBPP 55.5% pass@1. Synthetic-curriculum existence proof at exactly our scale band; contamination/benchmark-proximity controls are thin - treat as recipe inspiration, not matched-baseline evidence.

title: TinyGSM: achieving >80% on GSM8K with small language models
claim: 1.3B generator + 1.3B learned verifier reach 81.5% GSM8K from 12.3M GPT-3.5-synthetic problems, beating the teacher (77.4%); the verifier (best-of-k selection) carries a large share of the gain. Also cited in PARSE.md - re-verified this pass.

title: CodeRL: Mastering Code Generation through Pretrained Models and Deep Reinforcement Learning
claim: Actor-critic RL where the critic is trained on UNIT-TEST EXECUTION outcomes; extends CodeT5 (770M-class); SOTA on APPS + zero-shot SOTA on MBPP. Executor-as-reward at sub-1B scale; abstract does not give compute-matched baselines.

title: RLEF: Grounding Code LLMs in Execution Feedback with Reinforcement Learning
claim: End-to-end RL teaching models to USE execution feedback across turns: SOTA CodeContests at 8B and 70B with an order-of-magnitude fewer samples at inference. The executor's value compounds when the model is TRAINED to consume its feedback, not just gated by it - direct precedent for H-VL retry-policy training.

title: Beyond Human Data: Scaling Self-Training for Problem-Solving with Language Models (ReST-EM)
claim: EM-style loop: sample, filter by BINARY correctness feedback, finetune, repeat - PaLM-2 on MATH/APPS significantly surpasses finetuning on human data; gains scale with model size and DIMINISH after a few iterations (the plateau STP later attributes to statement supply).

title: Scaling Flaws of Verifier-Guided Search in Mathematical Reasoning
claim: Verifier-guided search with LEARNED verifiers (OVMs, PRMs) underperforms repeated sampling at large sample budgets across Mistral-7B/DeepSeekMath-7B on GSM8K/MATH - imperfect verifiers misrank and prune ALL valid paths, worst on hard/OOD items. Does NOT apply to sound verifiers on their covered slice; also verified in RAG.md and present in lit-KB (arxiv_2502.00271). Sound-checker loops fail by PLATEAU (data sparsity), learned-verifier loops fail by DECAY (precision).

title: Logic-LM: Empowering Large Language Models with Symbolic Solvers for Faithful Logical Reasoning
claim: LLM translates NL to symbolic form; deterministic solver executes; self-refinement on SOLVER ERROR MESSAGES; +39.2% over standard prompting / +18.4% over CoT across 5 logical-reasoning datasets. In lit-KB (arxiv_2305.12295). The executor's error channel is itself a training/refinement signal - adopt for H-PS.

title: SATNet: Bridging deep learning and logical reasoning using a differentiable satisfiability solver
claim: Differentiable MAXSAT layer; visual-Sudoku claim later shown to rest on label leakage (see satnet-debunk-2020). Carried from reports/lit-structured-parsing-and-inner-symbolic.md ([memory] there) - UNVERIFIED-THIS-PASS.

title: Assessing SATNet's Ability to Solve the Symbol Grounding Problem
claim: Without leaked intermediate symbol labels, visual SATNet drops to 0% test accuracy - the differentiable-logic layer never solved symbol grounding; naive SATNet on an MNIST grounding sanity check is worse than logic-free models. Carried: search-snippet quoting the NeurIPS abstract this pass + prior verification in the parsing report; proceedings page itself not fetched - UNVERIFIED-THIS-PASS at page level.

title: Kimina-Prover Preview: Towards Large Formal Reasoning Models with Reinforcement Learning
claim: 72B RL prover, 80.7% miniF2F at pass@8192 (search-result snippet only) - included solely as the sample-budget-inflation datapoint (pass@8192 vs pass@32 comparisons are not comparable). UNVERIFIED-THIS-PASS.

title: DeepSeek-Prover-V2: Advancing Formal Mathematical Reasoning via Reinforcement Learning for Subgoal Decomposition
claim: 671B prover, SFT'd from DeepSeek-V3-Base-671B ('We perform supervised fine-tuning on DeepSeek-V3-Base-671B', §2.3 via arxiv html fetch 2026-07-11) - i.e., an MoE-based system (~37B activated per token per the DeepSeek-V3 report, see deepseek-v3-2024), so 80x/167x total-parameter ratios vs 8B/4B challengers are NOT active-compute ratios; abstract reports 88.9% miniF2F-test at its own budget; its pass@32 figures used as comparison targets by Goedel-V2/Pythagoras come from those papers' tables. Abs + html pages fetched 2026-07-11 (revision pass).

title: LEVER: Learning to Verify Language-to-Code Generation with Execution
claim: Learned verifier over program+execution-result improves language-to-code selection. Carried from PARSE.md (verified there 2026-07-11); cited here only to bridge the execution-selection lever - UNVERIFIED-THIS-PASS.

title: DeepSeek-V3 Technical Report
claim: 'a strong Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) language model with 671B total parameters with 37B activated for each token' (abstract, fetched 2026-07-11 revision pass). Cited solely to qualify the DeepSeek-Prover-V2-671B comparator: total-parameter ratios (80x/167x) vs dense 8B/4B provers are not active-compute ratios (~5x/~9x on activated params).

title: Faith and Fate: Limits of Transformers on Compositionality
venue: NeurIPS 2023 [memory]; arXiv abs page shows no venue
claim: On multi-digit multiplication, logic-grid puzzles, and dynamic programming, transformers - INCLUDING models trained/finetuned on explicit step-by-step reasoning traces - solve compositional tasks by linearized subgraph matching (pattern reuse), not systematic procedure induction, and fail as complexity grows or OOD. Third independent negative line (with paradox-2022, prontoqa-2023) against trace-distillation-without-executor.
